postData.contentHtml:
Rugby fans worldwide are eagerly anticipating the next round of the 2026 Nations Championship, with a compelling clash between New Zealand and Italy scheduled for July 11. Kicking off at 6:10 a.m. BST, the match will take place at HNRY Stadium in New Zealand.

New Zealand enters this fixture on the back of a narrow victory against France in their previous outing, showcasing their enduring strength. Italy, however, will be looking to bounce back after a defeat to Japan. This sets the stage for a potentially gripping encounter, with Italy eager to prove their mettle against the mighty All Blacks.

For those keen to watch the action unfold, several options are available. Fans in the UK can access a free live stream of the match on ITVX. However, as ITVX is geo-restricted to the UK, international viewers will need to use a Virtual Private Network (VPN) to access the stream. Services like ExpressVPN are recommended to connect to a UK server, allowing seamless viewing from anywhere in the world. Other free streaming platforms include Rugbypass TV in the US, Virgin Media Play in Ireland, and 9Now for selected matches in Australia.

The 2026 Nations Championship is the inaugural edition of this exciting new biennial tournament, bringing together 12 of the world's top rugby nations – six from the Northern Hemisphere and six from the Southern Hemisphere. Teams play each other once across two international windows in July and November, culminating in a finals weekend at Twickenham on November 29. The tournament aims to add significant context and excitement to the international rugby calendar.

Social media sentiment surrounding the New Zealand vs. Italy match and the broader Nations Championship has been largely positive, with fans expressing excitement for the new format and the high-stakes matchups.
postData.title: How to Watch New Zealand vs. Italy in the 2026 Nations Championship Rugby: Live Stream Details
postData.summary: Get ready for a thrilling rugby clash! New Zealand faces Italy in the 2026 Nations Championship on July 11. Find out how to catch all the action, including free live stream options and how to bypass geo-restrictions with a VPN.
